risks: Storage accounts have networkAcls.defaultAction set to Allow, permitting unrestricted network access to training data and ML workspace storage
No compute resources (ML compute cluster or compute instance) are defined — actual training jobs cannot run without compute
No diagnostic settings resources to route audit logs from Key Vault, Storage, ACR, and ML workspace to Log Analytics — audit logging goal is only partially met
LRS redundancy on both storage accounts provides no zone or geo failover — data loss risk for training data and outputs
Basic ACR SKU does not support private endpoints or content trust, limiting production security hardening options
Public network access is enabled on ML workspace, Key Vault, storage accounts, and ACR — increases attack surface
No RBAC role assignments defined for ML workspace managed identity to access the Data Lake storage account
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure ML compute cluster resource (Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/computes) to enable training job execution
Add diagnostic settings resources (Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings) for each service to route logs to the Log Analytics workspace, fulfilling the audit logging requirement
Restrict storage account networkAcls.defaultAction to Deny and add appropriate IP/VNet exceptions
Add RBAC role assignments granting the ML workspace managed identity Storage Blob Data Contributor on the Data Lake account
Consider upgrading storage redundancy from LRS to ZRS for production-grade resilience
Add a resource tagging strategy for cost tracking and operational governance
Consider disabling local auth (shared key access) on storage accounts per Azure best practices
